name: single-hallucination
description: One trace where bot hallucinated a fake return policy. 1 occurrence in 200 traces. Could be one-off or systematic.

item.title: Hallucination detected in customer-support-bot response
item.description: User report: customer-support-bot cited a '90-day return policy' that does not exist. Actual policy is 30 days.
item.note:
Investigated the hallucination report. Findings:

- Confirmed hallucination in trace hall-6601 at 11:42:18 UTC
- The bot responded: "Our return policy allows returns within 90 days of purchase with a full refund."
- Actual company policy: 30-day return window (verified in knowledge base doc kb-returns-v3)
- The retrieval step DID return the correct document chunk (relevance score 0.88)
- The LLM ignored the retrieved context and generated a fabricated policy
- LLM output confidence score (from logprobs): 0.72 — lower than typical 0.85+ for factual responses

Scope assessment:
- Checked all 200 traces in the last 24 hours for similar patterns
- No other traces mention "90-day" or incorrect return policies
- Ran a broader check for low-confidence outputs (<0.75): found 4 traces, but manual review shows they were accurate
- The hallucination appears to be a single occurrence

However:
- 1 in 200 = 0.5% hallucination rate on a critical topic (company policy)
- Cannot rule out systematic risk — the LLM may hallucinate on other policy topics with different phrasing
- No guardrail or fact-checking step exists in the current agent pipeline
- A single hallucination on return policy could have financial/legal implications

This needs monitoring. One occurrence isn't enough to call it a pattern, but hallucinating company policy is high-stakes even as a one-off.

projectMemory:
## Project Agents
- **customer-support-bot**: Handles customer inquiries via RAG. Typical trace has 3-5 LLM calls, 1-2 retrieval steps. Normal latency: 2-4s per trace.
- **email-classifier**: Classifies incoming emails. Single LLM call per trace, ~500ms.

## Baselines
- Error rate: <2%
- Daily cost: ~$5
- Trace volume: ~200/day
